name: slack
context: Slack platform — messaging, bots, webhooks, Block Kit UI, and event-driven automation
info: The Slack ecosystem covers building integrations and automations for Slack workspaces. Agents can send and manage messages, build interactive bots with Bolt.js, configure incoming and outgoing webhooks, design rich Block Kit UIs, and subscribe to workspace events. The platform uses OAuth 2.0 for authentication, granular scopes for permissions, and supports both HTTP-based and Socket Mode connections. This tree is organized around the jobs an agent actually performs: sending messages, building bots, posting via webhooks, composing Block Kit layouts, and handling real-time events.
body:
# Slack Ecosystem

Slack is a messaging platform where agents send messages, build interactive bots, configure webhooks, design rich UIs with Block Kit, and respond to workspace events in real time. The Slack API provides programmatic access to workspaces, channels, users, and message history.

## Agent Jobs

The primary tasks an agent performs in this ecosystem:

- **Send messages** — post, update, delete, and schedule messages in channels and DMs. See [[slack/messages]].
- **Build bots** — create interactive bots with Bolt.js that listen for events, respond to slash commands, and handle button clicks. See [[slack/bots]].
- **Configure webhooks** — send notifications to channels using incoming webhooks without a full bot setup. See [[slack/webhooks]].
- **Design Block Kit UIs** — compose rich, interactive message layouts with blocks, elements, and composition objects. See [[slack/block-kit]].
- **Handle events** — subscribe to workspace events, verify request signatures, and process event payloads. See [[slack/events]].

## Authentication

Slack apps authenticate via OAuth 2.0. Bot tokens (`xoxb-`) are used for most API calls. User tokens (`xoxp-`) act on behalf of a user. App-level tokens (`xapp-`) are used for Socket Mode connections.

| Token Type | Prefix | Use Case |
|------------|--------|----------|
| Bot token | `xoxb-` | API calls on behalf of the app |
| User token | `xoxp-` | API calls on behalf of a user |
| App-level token | `xapp-` | Socket Mode, event connections |

## Scopes and Permissions

Slack uses granular OAuth scopes to control what an app can access:

| Scope | Permission |
|-------|------------|
| `chat:write` | Send messages to channels |
| `channels:read` | View channel info |
| `commands` | Register slash commands |
| `incoming-webhook` | Post via webhooks |
| `app_mentions:read` | Receive @mention events |
| `reactions:write` | Add emoji reactions |

## Libraries

- **@slack/bolt** — framework for building Slack apps with events, commands, and interactions
- **@slack/web-api** — low-level client for all Slack Web API methods
- **@slack/webhook** — simple client for incoming webhooks
- **@slack/socket-mode** — Socket Mode client for firewall-friendly connections

sections.title: API overview
sections.body: The Slack Web API provides HTTP methods for all workspace operations: posting messages (`chat.postMessage`), managing channels (`conversations.*`), uploading files (`files.upload`), and more. All methods accept a bot or user token. Rate limits apply per method and workspace. See [[slack/messages]] for messaging and [[slack/bots]] for interactive features.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.body: Create a Slack app at https://api.slack.com/apps. Install it to a workspace to get a bot token (`xoxb-`). Use OAuth 2.0 for distribution. App-level tokens (`xapp-`) enable Socket Mode for [[slack/bots]] and [[slack/events]]. Store tokens in environment variables — never commit them to source.

sections.title: Scopes and permissions
sections.body: Request only the scopes your app needs. `chat:write` for [[slack/messages]], `commands` for [[slack/bots]] slash commands, `incoming-webhook` for [[slack/webhooks]], `app_mentions:read` for [[slack/events]] mention events. Scopes are configured in the app dashboard and requested during OAuth installation.
